models_tested.notes: v0.9 Sonnet 4.6 runs (evals 0-4 without_skill dated 2026-03-14 / 2026-04-22) carried over for without_skill 0-4; with_skill 0-4 re-run at v1.0 on 2026-04-23; new evals 5/7/8/9 both configurations dated 2026-04-23.
v0.9 Opus 4.7 without_skill runs (evals 0-4 dated 2026-04-22) carried over; with_skill 0-4 re-run at v1.0 on 2026-04-23; new evals 5/7/8/9 both configurations dated 2026-04-23.

notes: Spec 25 v1.0 benchmark: 9 evals × 2 configurations × 2 models = 36 runs. K=4 new evals kept (5 noise-rejection, 7 cross-assistant-sync, 8 silent-contradiction, 9 min-char-audit); eval 6 environment-scope-labeling was dropped during the Phase 2 discrimination gate because both Opus 4.7 and Sonnet 4.6 baselines scored 5/5 without_skill. Without_skill entries for the kept new evals reuse the Phase 2 gate runs (not re-run in Phase 4) to avoid drift between gate and published benchmark. Without_skill entries for evals 0-4 carry over from the prior v0.9 benchmark — they don't depend on skill version. With_skill entries for all 9 evals on both models are fresh v1.0 runs dated 2026-04-23.

Evals 0, 1, 3, and 4 are non-discriminating on both models at v1.0 (pass_rate 1.0 on both with_skill and without_skill across both executors). They establish a correctness baseline for the simplest learning paths — basic write, skill-creation route, update-in-place, multi-config disambiguation — but do not contribute to the pass-rate delta. They are retained as regression sentinels.

On claude-opus-4-7 the v1.0 skill produces +11 pp pass-rate lift across 9 evals (was +0 at v0.9). Discriminating cells on Opus without_skill: eval 5 noise-rejection (4/5 — included npm-install noise), eval 7 cross-assistant-sync (4/5 — no reciprocal mirror rule), eval 8 silent-contradiction (4/5 — transparent replace without naming the conflict), eval 9 min-char-audit (2/5 — 494-char rule body + 45% trim in turn-2 + embedded incident narrative).

On claude-sonnet-4-6 the v1.0 skill produces +20 pp pass-rate lift across 9 evals (was +8 at v0.9). Discriminating cells on Sonnet without_skill: eval 2 multi-target-routing (3/5 — appended workflow to both configs instead of routing to a skill, skipped plan step), eval 5 noise-rejection (1/5 — captured all 3 obvious items), eval 7 (4/5), eval 8 (4/5), eval 9 (2/5).

Eval 9 (min-char-audit) is a two-turn eval — the first in this suite. Turn 1 tests whether the Plan-step audit fires unprompted (primary signal: rule text ≤ 200 chars); turn 2 follow-up asks the agent to evaluate its own rule with a pinned affirmation regex or bounded rewrite. Known false-negative: the v1.0 skill can still leave turn-1 rule text above 200 chars even after the audit step, so the primary signal fails despite the audit being attempted. The turn-2 affirmation assertion was tightened during PR review to require the entire response to be the pinned affirmation (not just the first non-whitespace line); Sonnet with_skill eval 9 was re-graded 4/5 → 3/5 under the tightened criterion.

Self-graded by executor for all runs — no separate analyzer pass. All v1.0 runs used live-workspace grading in isolated temp dirs with no access to skills/learn/SKILL.md for without_skill runs; with_skill runs read the v1.0 SKILL.md and any references its Process steps mark MANDATORY.
